title: Repository State, References, and Revision Algebra · History Defense
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for repository state, references, and revision algebra and requires the smallest safe repository-state and revision-query proof with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for repository-state and revision-query proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.
Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G89 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip. Competency probe: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.

stimulus.title: Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A clean working tree is a complete forensic baseline.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.

options.text: Accept the Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline result because visible repository-state and revision-query proof output is the highest authority. This does not resolve diagnose the hidden failure.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline without its stated preconditions, omit the repository-state and revision-query proof trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline for repository-state and revision-query proof.
Run the Gita Readonly Forensic Baseline boundary case for repository-state and revision-query proof, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
